title: PNG backend text anchoring inconsistent with SVG
description: SkiaBackend::draw_text and draw_rotated_text interpret position.y as the top of the line box (cosmic_text default), while SvgBackend::draw_text emits SVG <text> where y is the baseline. Result: regenerated PNG snapshots after the layout overhaul show titles overlapping the plot area on top and X-axis titles clipped at the bottom canvas edge. SVG snapshots are visually correct. Fix: change SkiaBackend draw_text to subtract the line baseline offset (cosmic_text Buffer::layout_runs first run line_y, fallback font_size * 0.85) so position.y is the baseline. draw_rotated_text needs the same shift composed with the rotation transform: pre-translate buffer by (0, -ascent) so the baseline is the rotation pivot. After this lands, regenerate PNG snapshots only — SVG output is unchanged.
close_reason: Skia draw_text and draw_rotated_text now anchor at baseline matching SVG semantics, via line_y from cosmic_text Buffer::layout_runs (font_size*0.85 fallback)

title: Graph Margins
description: The current margin method is completely invalid. We need a way to account for needed space outside the graph render area so we can define the graph render area allowing for titles, axis labels, legends etc as well as long text or number ticks and things like color map range definitions. It should be highly modular and essentially invisible to the user, defined by what the user wants in the graph and automated from that. Want title, more top margin and a good placement etc. Tick string too long? more margin to avoid overlap etc
close_reason: Modular LayoutBuilder replaces hardcoded margins; legend/colormap reservations supported

title: bug(backend): disable antialiasing on axis-aligned straight lines (grids, ticks, box edges)
description:
Per .spec/LEARN.md the renderers should NOT antialias horizontal/vertical hairlines — they soften crisp 1-pixel grid + tick + axis lines and make charts look fuzzy at small sizes. We're currently antialiasing every path uniformly because there's no auto-detection.

Need: a path-classifier in the layer-1 backend trait (or in PathStyle) that checks each path command sequence and if it consists entirely of axis-aligned segments (LineTo where dx==0 || dy==0, no curves), drops antialiasing for that draw call. Both SkiaBackend and SvgBackend need to honour the hint.

Heuristic: classify at draw_path time, not at path build time, so the optimization is automatic for everything (grid, ticks, box outlines, whisker stems, candlestick wicks, etc.) without callers having to opt in.

Acceptance: re-render the gallery; grid lines and box outlines should look crisp at 100% zoom in the resulting PNGs. Minor backend-level fix; touches PathStyle interpretation in starsight-layer-1/src/backends/{rasters,vectors}.rs.
close_reason: Path::is_axis_aligned() classifier added in starsight-layer-1/src/paths.rs walks the command list, returns true iff every LineTo is purely horizontal/vertical and any QuadTo/CubicTo is rejected. Implicit close-segment also checked. SkiaBackend::draw_path sets paint.anti_alias=false on classifier hit; SkiaBackend::fill_rect always disables AA (rects are axis-aligned by construction). SvgBackend::draw_path emits shape-rendering=crispEdges on classifier hit; SvgBackend::fill_rect always emits it. 7 new unit tests cover empty path, horizontal run, vertical run, orthogonal box (with proper close), diagonal rejection, cubic rejection, close-with-implicit-diagonal rejection. ~37 layer-5 snapshots regenerate; diffs limited to the new attribute on axis-aligned paths/rects. Gallery refreshed: scatter dots stay smooth (curves keep AA), grid + axes + legend border render crisp.

title: bug(candlestick): first bar centered on y-axis, half-body is outside the plot
description:
examples/scientific/candlestick.png shows the first OHLC at timestamp=0.0 with its body extending from x=-half to x=+half — but timestamp=0.0 maps to area.left exactly, so the left half of the body sits *outside* the plot area. Visually the first candle is clipped or floats over the y-axis line.

Likely fix: the LinearScale on the x-axis treats the data range [t_min, t_max] as the plot area's [left, right] edges. CandlestickMark needs to either:
(a) inset the data range by half a band, so timestamp=t_min maps to area.left + half_width and timestamp=t_max maps to area.right - half_width (matches BarMark's band-center semantics); or
(b) extend the data_extent it reports by half a band on each side so the figure leaves room for the body width.

Option (b) is more honest with the figure's auto-axis logic — the candle bodies are half a band wide, so the data_extent should reflect that.
close_reason: data_extent in marks/candlestick.rs:196 now insets the reported x range by half a band on each side (median timestamp spacing × 0.5). Single-row case uses ±0.5. Two snapshots regenerate; example PNG visually confirmed first/last candles fit inside the plot rect.

title: bug: waterfall_bar example renders all bars overlapping at one position
description:
examples/showcases/waterfall_bar.rs adds 10 separate BarMark instances (one per stage), each with x.len()=1. With the orientation-aware data extent + category axis introduced in 27d40f4, category_labels() returns only the first mark's labels, so Axis::category builds a [0,1] domain and each BarMark renders its single bar at i=0 within that one band — i.e. all 10 bars stack on top of each other at the plot midpoint.

Predates this branch (the same issue is visible in commit 3376246 before the layout overhaul). Two viable fixes:

1. Make BarMark accept per-bar bases — change base: Option<f64> to base: Option<Vec<f64>> on BarMark and have render_bar use the per-index base. Then waterfall is one BarMark with 10 labels, 10 values, 10 bases. API breaking but correct.

2. Make category_labels() aggregate labels across all bar marks (Vec<String> from concatenation), and have BarMark::render know its global offset within the merged category axis. More complex; mixes mark-local data with figure-level state.

Recommend option 1 — cleaner API, simpler render path, and the waterfall semantic (each bar floats at its own running total) is exactly what per-bar base expresses.

Out of scope for the visual-output deep-dive branch; pulled out as its own follow-up.
notes: Expanded scope (design 2026-04-28): also add connector lines (thin gray, drawn between consecutive bars at y = bases[i] + y[i]) per spec #37. Currency labels deferred (proper TextMark work). API change is breaking — BarMark.base: Option<f64> → bases: Option<Vec<f64>>, BarMark.color: Option<Color> → colors: Option<Vec<Color>> (with single/plural convenience builders), plus new connectors: bool field. Bumps next release to 0.3.0. Plan file: ~/.claude/plans/create-a-bd-issue-declarative-spindle.md
close_reason: Implemented in commit 05564b6 (branch 0.3.0). API extensions for BarMark per-bar bases/colors/connectors, PointMark per-point colors/radii/alpha, HeatmapMark log_scale; waterfall_bar rewritten; bubble_scatter, movie_heatmap, laser_plasma examples added. All snapshots green, clippy clean, xtask gallery 23/23 OK.

title: Edge gutter for tick label overhang
description: After replacing the hardcoded right_margin=20 with the LayoutBuilder, no built-in component reserves on the right side or on the top (when no title). The rightmost X tick label is centered on plot.right and the topmost Y tick label is centered on plot.top, so each can overhang the canvas edge by up to half its label width/height. Fix: have XTickLabelsComponent return a second Reservation on the Right side sized as max_label_width / 2 + small buffer, and YTickLabelsComponent return a second Reservation on Top sized as max_label_height / 2 + small buffer. Composes correctly with future colorbar component on the right.

title: bug(examples): violin and boxplot examples are subpar
description:
examples/composition/violin.rs and examples/composition/box_plot.rs work but don't sell the marks well:

- The data is too tidy/synthetic. Real distributions have asymmetric tails, bimodality, or clear outliers; the current samples are uniformly increasing across cohorts which makes the densities all look like the same shape shifted up. The reader can't see what's *interesting* about each mark.

- box_plot.png has visible outlier dots only on placebo and high-dose; the inner two cohorts look identical to each other. Should pick a domain (e.g. ad campaign click-throughs, lab measurement noise, ML model accuracy across seeds) where each group's spread tells a different story.

- violin.png inner box overlay almost touches the violin envelope on narrow violins — looks cramped. Either give the inner box smaller half-width (~0.10 instead of 0.18) or shrink it relative to the densest violin.

Don't refactor the marks themselves — this is example-content quality.
close_reason: Replaced monotonically-increasing synthetic data in examples/composition/box_plot.rs and violin.rs with deterministic but realistic distributions: box plot has placebo (tight unimodal, no outliers), low dose (wider IQR + 1 high outlier), med dose (2 low outliers + slight skew), high dose (narrow IQR + 1 extreme outlier). Violin has Q1 unimodal, Q2 bimodal, Q3 right-skewed long tail, Q4 narrow peaked — densities now read as distinct shapes instead of shifted copies. Inner-box factor in marks/violin.rs:render_full bumped from 0.18 to 0.12 so the inner box doesn't crowd narrow violins. Snapshots regenerated; gallery PNGs visually verified.

title: bug(pie): chart shows numeric axes/grid, slice labels don't carry the slice color
description:
Two related issues on PieMark:

1. The placeholder unit-square data_extent makes Figure draw a 0..1 numeric x/y axis with grid + tick labels around the pie. Pies are angular charts; the axis decoration is visual noise and confuses readers. (See related: starsight-yrp.2 — that one proposes the mechanism; this one is the concrete bug to fix.)

2. The midpoint slice labels render in a single label_color (default BLACK). Reading 'Solar' over the green slice and 'Other' over the dark purple slice both in black is fine on light slices but unreadable on dark slices. Either auto-pick a contrast color per slice (luminance-based threshold), or render slice labels in the slice color outside the slice instead of inside.

Surfaced reviewing the 0.3.0 examples: see examples/composition/pie.png and donut.png. Land both fixes in the same PR — they share render path.
close_reason: Mark trait gains wants_axes() default true; PieMark overrides to false. Figure::render_to skips render_grid_lines + render_axes only when EVERY mark on the figure returns false (mixed charts keep axes). PieMark::render auto-picks label color via Rec.601 luminance threshold (white text when slice luminance < 0.5, black otherwise); explicit .label_color() override still wins. Pie/donut snapshots regenerate; example PNGs visually verified — clean wedges with no axis chrome and readable labels on every slice.

title: Implement laser-plasma phase-space showcase, single-panel (spec #7)
description:
Implement showcase example #7 (Laser-plasma phase space, .spec/SHOWCASE_INPUTS.md:102-130). Single-panel form for 0.2.0; full multi-panel deferred to 0.4.0 (GridLayout).

## Why
0.2.0 spec gap. Demonstrates HeatmapMark with a domain-realistic dataset (stimulated Raman scattering) and the new log-scale color axis.

## What
- New example: examples/scientific/laser_plasma.rs. Picks the middle snapshot (delta=0.15) of the spec's three-snapshot sequence. 200×200 grid in [(159.0, 160.0) × (-5, 5)] of f_e(x,p) = n0/sqrt(2π T_e) · exp(-p²/(2 T_e)) · (1 + delta·cos(k_L·x)). viridis colormap, log scale.

## Depends on
HeatmapMark log-scale color path (tracked in the spec #16 issue). Land #16's API work first.

## Acceptance
- laser_plasma PNG: vertical Gaussian-in-p shape, modulated horizontally by the cos(k_L x) ripple, log-scale viridis.
- Registered in examples/Cargo.toml + README.md.

title: Implement bubble scatter showcase + PointMark per-point colors/radii (spec #3)

title: polish(pie/donut): suppress placeholder 0..1 axes/grid for pie-only figures
description: PieMark currently returns a unit-square data_extent so the figure's merged_extent doesn't reject pie-only charts as 'No data to render'. As a side effect, the figure draws Wilkinson ticks + grid lines at 0/0.25/0.5/0.75/1 which is visible noise around what should be a clean pie. Two ways to fix: (a) extend Figure with a .hide_axes() / .axis_visibility(...) builder so users opt in to axisless mode; (b) detect when every mark in a figure returns None for data_extent (or all marks are pie-like) and skip render_axes + render_grid_lines automatically. Either is reasonable; (b) is more magical but matches the 'figure picks sensible defaults' philosophy. Surfaced during 0.3.0 PieMark/DonutMark work — examples/composition/pie.png + donut.png show the issue.
close_reason: Mark trait gains wants_axes() default true; PieMark overrides to false. Figure::render_to skips render_grid_lines + render_axes only when EVERY mark on the figure returns false (mixed charts keep axes). PieMark::render auto-picks label color via Rec.601 luminance threshold (white text when slice luminance < 0.5, black otherwise); explicit .label_color() override still wins. Pie/donut snapshots regenerate; example PNGs visually verified — clean wedges with no axis chrome and readable labels on every slice.

title: polish(legend): contrasting border so the swatch box is visible against the plot area
description: The legend currently renders as a translucent white rect (theme.background with alpha 230) at the top-right of the plot area. On charts where the plot's background also reads as white-ish (the default light theme; many heatmap palettes with bright top-right cells), the legend's right and bottom edges visually blend into the data and there's no cue for where it cuts off the chart. Add a 1-pixel stroke in theme.axis (or theme.tick_label) around the legend rect so the box's footprint is explicit; pair it with a small inset shadow or higher-contrast fill if needed. Verify against light + dark themes by re-running cargo xtask gallery and checking heatmap_basic / scatter / mixed-mark legend snapshots.

title: xtask gallery: build-then-run instead of cargo run --release per example
description:
## Why
`cargo xtask gallery` currently iterates over each [[example]] in examples/Cargo.toml and shells out to `cargo run --release --example <name>`. With ~22 examples, the per-invocation cargo overhead (metadata + dep-graph check, even on cache hit) plus serial execution makes a clean run take several minutes. xtask/src/gallery.rs:33-58 is the loop.

## What
Replace the per-example `cargo run` with one upfront `cargo build --release --examples --manifest-path examples/Cargo.toml` (parallel build via cargo's normal job server) followed by direct exec of each `target/release/examples/<name>` binary. Cargo handles parallelism; per-example overhead drops from ~2s to ~10ms, and we no longer pay the metadata cost on cache hits.

## Acceptance
- `cargo xtask gallery` runs in roughly the time of `cargo build --release --examples` plus the runtime of the rendering passes themselves (a fraction of current).
- Behavior identical: same PNGs produced in target/gallery/examples/, same failure surface (a missing PNG after running still flags the example).
- Exit code unchanged on per-example failures (collect failures, bail at end with same message).

## Notes
Surfaced during 0.3.0 showcase work (~4 minute clean gallery rebuild on dev workstation).
close_reason: Replaced per-example 'cargo run --release --example' with a single upfront 'cargo build --release --examples' followed by direct binary exec at target/release/examples/<name>. Cargo overhead per example dropped from ~2s to ~0s; total gallery time on dev workstation now bound by render time (~2m45s on 23 examples).

title: Document Axis::category category-axis bypass behavior
description: Bar marks bypass the scale on the category axis and use band-center math directly (area.left + (i + 0.5) * band_width). Axis::category has tick_positions at band edges, used only for grid lines (which intentionally fall between bars). The N+1 empty tick_labels are aligned with N+1 tick_positions to honor the 'one label per position' contract. Fix: add a doc comment on Axis::category making both invariants explicit, so a future contributor doesn't try to iterate tick_labels for categories.
